workflow.name: research-critic
workflow.instruction: Run a reusable Researcher A / Researcher B critic loop. Keep this workflow neutral and reusable across callers. Use the run directory convention from the generic workflow interpreter: baton.json and history.md at --run-dir, worker/approval outputs under --run-dir/outputs when persisted by the caller.

input.prompt: Normalize the task input, inspect available evidence, and produce a research packet. Return ready_for_attack when the packet is ready for critic review, needs_input when user answers are required, or blocked when progress is unsafe without external input. If revising after critic feedback or user answers, preserve answered decisions and close only evidence-backed questions.

Ask the user only the blocking research questions from the latest available draft-producing output and latest research_attack output. Prefer draft-producing outputs in this deterministic freshness order: research_revision, then research_answered_draft, then research_draft. Continue with approval approved after the user answer is captured in the approval output; use blocked only when the user cannot or will not provide required input.

Revise the research packet using the approved user answers from ask_research_questions and any critic feedback if present. Preserve answered decisions and close only evidence-backed questions. Return ready_for_attack when the packet is ready for critic review, needs_input when more user answers are required, or blocked when progress is unsafe without external input.

Attack the latest available research packet for weak evidence, unsafe assumptions, missing user input, misclassified blockers, and over-broad candidate approaches. Prefer draft-producing outputs in this deterministic freshness order: research_revision, then research_answered_draft, then research_draft. Return approved only when the packet is ready for downstream handoff, needs_revision when a bounded revision should loop through a fresh draft-producing output, needs_input when user answers are required, or blocked for an unsafe stop.

Revise the latest available research packet using the latest research_attack verdict and any approved user answers. Prefer draft-producing outputs in this deterministic freshness order: research_revision, then research_answered_draft, then research_draft. Preserve answered decisions, address each bounded critic finding, and return ready_for_attack when the packet is ready for another critic review, needs_input when user answers are required, or blocked when progress is unsafe without external input.

Finalize persistence metadata for the approved research packet using the latest approved research packet and latest research_attack verdict. Prefer draft-producing outputs in this deterministic freshness order: research_revision, then research_answered_draft, then research_draft. The caller should save concrete worker outputs under the active run directory's outputs/ folder and persist the returned baton/history with the generic persist helper. Return saved after emitting artifact/result references to the saved packet, or blocked if the packet cannot be safely saved.
Research workflow is blocked. Surface the blocker and required input.
